texts.full_text: appellants' concern that such an interpretation is impractical because it requires employers to keep detailed records of each employee's working time during 'irregular' work schedules, we note again that in Huddleston this court required all hours worked to be recorded regardless of whether they were considered regular or irregular. Thereby the employees received their full due and overtime compensation was paid where due. 9 We conclude that an employer may establish a bona fide agreement with his employees which provides for payment at straight time in excess of minimum wage for work performed on weekends and holidays, regardless of whether such work is designated as regular or irregular by either party.
